name: Banana Bread
description: A cross between banana bread and a drizzle cake, this easy banana loaf is a quick bake that can be frozen un-iced. Great for using up overripe bananas.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: 2–3 tsp, to achieve a runny consistency

steps.title: Prepare the tin
steps.content: Heat the oven to 180°C / 160°C fan / Gas 4. Butter a 2lb loaf tin and line the base and sides with baking parchment.

steps.title: Cream butter and sugar
steps.content: Cream {0001} and {0002} until light and fluffy, then slowly add {0003} with a little of {0004}.

steps.title: Bake
steps.content: Pour the mixture into the prepared tin and bake for about 50 minutes, or until cooked through. Check the loaf at 5-minute intervals from around 30–40 minutes in by testing with a skewer (it should come out clean), as the time may vary depending on the shape of your loaf tin.

steps.title: Cool
steps.content: Cool in the tin for 10 minutes, then remove to a wire rack.

steps.title: Make the icing
steps.content: Mix {0007} with {0008} to make a runny icing.

steps.title: Ice and decorate
steps.content: Drizzle the icing across the top of the cake and decorate with {0009}.
